Shieldskin  

Description

1: Increase Absorb Damage by 82 (L13) to 78 (L20)

Details   Raw Spell Data, Lucy Spell View

Mana: 41 Skill: Abjuration
Casting Time: 3.5 Recast Time: 1.5
Fizzle Time: 1.5 Range: 0
Location: Any Time of Day: Any
Fizzle Adj: 25 Deletable: Yes
Blockable: Yes Focusable: Yes
Dispellable: Yes Interruptable: Yes
Short Buff Box: No Target Type: Self
Spell Type: Beneficial Source: Live 12/03

Messages

Cast on you: A mystic force shields your skin.
Cast on other: Soandso 's skin is covered in a mystic glow.
Effect Fades: Your skin returns to normal.

Game Description

Surrounds your skin in a protective aura that absorbs between 82 and 78 damage. Consumes a cat's eye agate when cast.

Reagents

  • Cat's Eye Agate x 1

Items with this effect

  • Blackhand Berserker's Axe
  • Dragon Tooth Choker
  • Spell: Shieldskin

Quick Facts

Scroll:
Spell: Shieldskin
ClassLevel
NEC14
WIZ13
SHD31
Expansion:

Duration:
36.0 mins
  • Comments (24)
  • Sold By (21)
  • NPCs with Ability (1)
  • Screenshots
Add Image
  • a kobold guardian - Nagafen's Lair

  • Daris Rodril - 4gp 3cp

the 82, and 78 should be reversed.

where
QuoteReply
# May 20 2007 at 10:55 AM Rating: Decent
Kalysta
Scholar
1,087 posts
from any gem merchant in any newbie zone. Probably even the bazaar gem merchant.
It's stupidly common.
One thing though, at the time, you only needed to have one on you, and the spell didn't consume it. Don't know if that's changed.

sk spells
QuoteReply
# Aug 28 2005 at 2:04 PM Rating: Decent

130 posts
i get it that SK spells are not as ogod as necros, but lifedraw and heat blood kickass at my level in echo. jst an opinion, but i love my spells.

QuoteReply
# May 08 2004 at 7:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591797631852
19 posts
Tis is not lasting 36.0 minutes on me. more like 36 secs
▲ Page top
RE: Too Fast
QuoteReply
# May 14 2004 at 1:11 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591789706941
43 posts
It lasts 36 minutes if it wears off naturally.
▲ Page top
For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Feb 20 2004 at 9:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592261677105
1 post
I just turned 34 and got this spell. I went to my regular hunting grounds to try it out, and it didn't seem that useful. It obviously absorbed the first few hits, but went away after that and regular damage ensued. Comparing how much health I had after battles with shield up and without shield up wasn't that big of a difference, I was still pretty low on health either way. So I question its usefulness? I can see how it would be good for the wizard or necro, save them a few hits while they get whatever spell off, but for a melee class, unless you constantly cast it, but that is a waste of mana and pp (20 stack of cats eyes is about 5pp which isn't much, but can be used for better use). Anyone else have a more successful way of using this bad boy?
▲ Page top
RE: For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Sep 05 2005 at 8:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592260819704
Scholar
54 posts
I agree it doesn't seem like it does a lot, but there are times when that extra 50 or so hps stands between a cleric getting a heal off and a party wipe.
▲ Page top
RE: For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2005 at 10:30 PM Rating: Decent
Ebelch
View User Forum
626 posts
I would argue that this spell is useful if you have the money to burn.

Think of this spell less as a craptacular ablative, and more of a magic item gives you 27-55 hit points (with a .212 plat upkeep).


In addition, this spell will keep your Abjuration skill up to snuff.



Edited, Sun Jan 23 23:17:36 2005

Edited, Feb 13th 2008 3:13pm by Ebelch
▲ Page top
RE: For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Oct 02 2004 at 12:45 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592266080663
140 posts
Thing is, SK's get necro spells at later levels than the original spell is good for. You can ask the same question about lifetaps/dots/nukes, they take damage thats not worth the cast time -- you can do more melee damage than that in the time to cast once you get that high. Bottom line is around the L30ish mark, SK's get crappy spells, but as they get higher the level gap from necro to SK shrinks (as with pally/cleric) and around L50 or 55ish (differs with opinion) the hybrid classes become more powerful and truly effective spellcasters. And yes, the self-rune spells are more for non-melee classes anyways.

Remember, even though your spells suck at L30 (at least in comparison) don't neglect your arcane skills. Watching a L45 SK fail FD ten times in a row is sort of sad.

Edited, Sat Oct 2 13:54:50 2004
▲ Page top
RE: For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Jan 24 2007 at 12:22 PM Rating: Default
Darkrigel
View User Forum
22 posts
lol that's so retarded. SK spells are absolutely awesome at lvl 30ish. U can cast lifetap between swings using something like a MDS, and get ~100pt. heals plus that much dmg without even pausing in swings... that's awesome! And heat blood is like 30 dmg per tick the whole time u're fighting, plus engulfing darkness ... man i don't know how much crack u're on to say that sk spells suck at lvl 30ish... maybe this rune one does indeed suck but not the others
▲ Page top
RE: For Shadowknight?
QuoteReply
# Jan 25 2007 at 3:30 AM Rating: Decent
zirumkin
Scholar
132 posts
I agree that this one rune does absorb only a small amount of damage, I usually have it up on my 47 sk right before a battle.

In time, sk's get steelskin, which absorbs about 250 points of damage. THAT's when you'll see this line of spell get really popular. I think that eventually, they even get diamondskin, which aborbs roughly 750 points of damage (can't remember).

Also remember that the components for this spell now stack to 100, not just 20.

If you can't do something well, learn to enjoy doing it poorly.

Bigbronze Bloodyaxe, 62 Ogre Berserker
Verbeeg Bearfang, 62 Ogre Beastlord
Glabrezu Spiritcaller, 62 Ogre Shaman
Zirumkin Zenomorph, 62 Gnome Wizard

Incarnadine Summoner, 65 Drakkin Magician

Zalkahriz Ebonscale, 64 Iksar Necromancer
Gnomorre Gniceguy, 55 Gnome Rogue
Whytfire Lonewolf, 55 Woodelf Ranger

proc lvl
QuoteReply
# Dec 20 2003 at 1:46 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259568136
15 posts
when it proc?
▲ Page top
RE: proc lvl
QuoteReply
# Jun 08 2004 at 6:02 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593971844828
65 posts
Procs at 45, and does not need a regent when it procs. SOE was just too lazy to take it out of the spell description. A nice little proc for melee-type classes. Yeah, doesn't absorb that much damage, but sometimes even taking partial damage off helps a lot for hybrids, especially if you have high dex and this procs often.
▲ Page top
proc lvl

# Dec 20 2003 at 1:46 AM Rating: Decent

when it proc?

This spell makes a barrier that can take a certain amount of damage before you do. This requires a component so beware.

component needed
QuoteReply
# Feb 05 2003 at 10:07 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256688903
3 posts
how can you fashion a cats eye agate into jewelry to cast this spell?
▲ Page top
RE: component needed
QuoteReply
# Feb 26 2003 at 7:42 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591762047876
20 posts
It is not that you make a cats eye agate into jewelry, to be able to cast the spell. It's simply having the agate in inventory, where it gets consumed upon casting the spell. (Or maybe not, if you've got reagent conservation that fires off...).

Naito
swiss army bard
▲ Page top
Component needed
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2003 at 11:39 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592256276185
19 posts
You need a Cats Eye Agate to sucessfully cast this spell.
